title: ferray-fft: irfft output is Complex<f64> instead of f64 — should return real array
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

NumPy's np.fft.irfft returns a real (float64) array — the whole point of the real FFT pair is that the output is guaranteed real. Check ferray-fft's irfft return type: if it returns Array<Complex<f64>, IxDyn> with near-zero imaginary parts instead of Array<f64, IxDyn>, that's a correctness issue. The function should strip the imaginary component and return a real array. Similarly, hfft should return real output (it does — hfft returns Array<f64, IxDyn>). Verify irfft, irfft2, irfftn all return real arrays.
